title: 3 European Growth Companies With Up To 30% Insider Ownership
content:
As European markets experience a positive shift, with the STOXX Europe 600 Index rising by 3.92% amid hopes for a shorter-lived Middle East conflict, investors are increasingly focused on identifying growth opportunities in this evolving landscape. In such an environment, companies that exhibit strong insider ownership often signal confidence from those who know the business best and can be appealing options for those seeking to align with management interests.

Top 10 Growth Companies With High Insider Ownership In Europe

(OM:CLAV) 18% 116.2% Circus (XTRA:CA1) 21.9% 88.1% Bonesupport Holding (OM:BONEX) 10.2% 34% Bergen Carbon Solutions (OB:BCS) 10.2% 55.5%

Click here to see the full list of 214 stocks from our Fast Growing European Companies With High Insider Ownership screener.

Let's uncover some gems from our specialized screener.

SP Group

Simply Wall St Growth Rating: ★★★★☆☆

Overview: SP Group A/S, with a market cap of DKK4.29 billion, manufactures and sells moulded plastic and composite components across Denmark, Europe, the Americas, Asia, the Middle East, Australia, and Africa.

Operations: The company's revenue is primarily derived from its Plastics & Rubber segment, which generated DKK2.95 billion.

Insider Ownership: 13.5%

SP Group, trading significantly below its estimated fair value, shows potential for growth despite limited recent insider buying. The company reported modest earnings growth of 1.6% last year and forecasts a robust annual profit growth rate of 21.3%, outpacing the Danish market average. However, revenue is expected to grow at a slower pace than profits. Recent buybacks and confirmed guidance for substantial revenue increases in 2026 indicate strategic efforts to enhance shareholder value amidst high debt levels.

Click here and access our complete growth analysis report to understand the dynamics of SP Group. According our valuation report, there's an indication that SP Group's share price might be on the cheaper side.CPSE:SPG Ownership Breakdown as at Apr 2026

Framery Group Oyj

Simply Wall St Growth Rating: ★★★★★☆

Overview: Framery Group Oyj designs, manufactures, and markets soundproof private workspaces and related software solutions to address noise and privacy issues in open offices across Europe, the Middle East, Africa, the Americas, and Asia Pacific; it has a market cap of €494.84 million.

Story Continues

Operations: Framery Group Oyj generates its revenue primarily from the Furniture & Fixtures segment, which accounts for €222.13 million.

Insider Ownership: 12.2%

Framery Group Oyj, trading well below its estimated fair value, demonstrates strong growth potential with forecasted annual earnings growth of 22.9%, surpassing the Finnish market average. Despite a high debt level and slower revenue growth at 5.6% annually, insider activity shows more buying than selling recently. The company reported increased full-year sales to €222.13 million but saw a decline in quarterly net income to €3.19 million compared to last year’s figures, reflecting mixed financial performance amidst strategic leadership changes.

Get an in-depth perspective on Framery Group Oyj's performance by reading our analyst estimates report here. The valuation report we've compiled suggests that Framery Group Oyj's current price could be inflated.HLSE:FRAMERY Earnings and Revenue Growth as at Apr 2026

Himalaya Shipping

Simply Wall St Growth Rating: ★★★★★☆

Overview: Himalaya Shipping Ltd. offers dry bulk shipping services globally and has a market cap of NOK6 billion.

Operations: The company's revenue segment consists of $131.90 million from transportation - shipping services.

Insider Ownership: 30.1%

Himalaya Shipping shows promising growth potential, with earnings expected to grow significantly at 26.9% annually, outpacing the Norwegian market. Despite slower revenue growth at 8.4%, its insider ownership remains stable without substantial recent trading activity. Recent financials reveal a strong Q4 performance, with revenue rising to US$42.1 million from US$29.6 million year-on-year and net income increasing markedly to US$13.5 million from US$1.1 million, indicating robust operational momentum despite dividend reductions earlier in the year.

Dive into the specifics of Himalaya Shipping here with our thorough growth forecast report. Insights from our recent valuation report point to the potential overvaluation of Himalaya Shipping shares in the market.OB:HSHP Earnings and Revenue Growth as at Apr 2026

content:

In this fluctuating environment, growth companies with high insider ownership can offer potential resilience and alignment of interests between management and shareholders, making them intriguing options for investors looking to navigate these uncertain times.

Vimian Group

Vimian Group's earnings are projected to grow significantly at 28% annually, surpassing the Swedish market. Operations: The company's revenue is primarily generated from its activities in discovering, developing, and commercializing drugs, amounting to CHF232.51 million.

Insider Ownership: 24.5%

Idorsia is poised for significant growth, with earnings forecasted to expand 64.6% annually as it transitions to profitability within three years. Despite a volatile share price and past shareholder dilution, its revenue growth of 19.1% outpaces the Swiss market average. Recent developments include a Phase 3 program for lucerastat targeting Fabry disease and an exclusive agreement with EMS S.A. to distribute QUVIVIQ in Latin America, highlighting Idorsia's strategic expansion efforts and innovative pipeline potential.

title: 3 European Stocks Estimated To Be Trading Below Their Intrinsic Value In February 2026
content:
As the European markets show signs of modest recovery, with the pan-European STOXX Europe 600 Index ending slightly higher due to earnings optimism, investors are keenly observing opportunities amidst mixed performances across major indices. In this environment, identifying stocks that may be trading below their intrinsic value can offer potential for growth, making it crucial to consider companies with strong fundamentals and resilience in uncertain economic climates.

Top 10 Undervalued Stocks Based On Cash Flows In Europe

Click here to see the full list of 212 stocks from our Undervalued European Stocks Based On Cash Flows screener.

We're going to check out a few of the best picks from our screener tool.

Himalaya Shipping

Overview: Himalaya Shipping Ltd. offers dry bulk shipping services globally and has a market cap of NOK4.83 billion.

Operations: The company generates revenue of $119.38 million from its dry bulk shipping transportation services worldwide.

Estimated Discount To Fair Value: 36.1%

Himalaya Shipping appears undervalued based on cash flows, trading 36.1% below its fair value estimate of NOK162.04, with shares currently at NOK103.6. Although profit margins have decreased from 21.9% to 4.4%, the company secured a lucrative time charter for Mount Elbrus at US$30,000 per day until June 2026, enhancing cash flow stability. Despite revenue growth forecasts of only 13.2% annually, earnings are expected to grow significantly by over 64% per year.

In light of our recent growth report, it seems possible that Himalaya Shipping's financial performance will exceed current levels. Delve into the full analysis health report here for a deeper understanding of Himalaya Shipping.OB:HSHP Discounted Cash Flow as at Feb 2026

Kitron

Overview: Kitron ASA is an electronics manufacturing services provider operating across several countries including Norway, Sweden, and the United States, with a market cap of NOK18.03 billion.

Operations: The company generates revenue of €665.20 million from its Electronics Manufacturing Services segment across various international markets.

Estimated Discount To Fair Value: 49.8%

Kitron is trading at NOK82.45, significantly below its fair value estimate of NOK164.17, and more than 20% below future cash flow value. With revenue expected to grow 22% annually and earnings projected to increase by 32.9%, Kitron's financial outlook is robust relative to the Norwegian market. Recent substantial orders totaling EUR 147 million in the defense sector bolster its growth prospects, aligning with strategic ambitions for revenue expansion towards EUR 1 billion and beyond.

Story Continues

Our comprehensive growth report raises the possibility that Kitron is poised for substantial financial growth. Navigate through the intricacies of Kitron with our comprehensive financial health report here.OB:KIT Discounted Cash Flow as at Feb 2026

adidas

Operations: adidas generates revenue from its athletic and sports lifestyle products across Europe, Greater China, Japan, South Korea, Latin America, North America, and other international markets.

Estimated Discount To Fair Value: 30.3%

adidas is trading at €149.15, significantly below its estimated future cash flow value of €213.97 and 30.3% under its fair value estimate. Earnings are projected to grow annually by 18.6%, outpacing the German market's forecast of 16.3%. A recent €1 billion share buyback program, funded by anticipated strong cash flows in 2026, underscores adidas's financial health and commitment to enhancing shareholder value through strategic capital allocation.
